statement: EU policy has been a significant factor in Icelandic coalition formation since 2007. The 2009–2013 coalition (Samfylkingin + Left-Greens) applied for EU membership despite the Left-Greens' ambivalence, reflecting the crisis-era consensus. The 2013–2016 coalition (Independence Party + Progressive Party) suspended negotiations as a core coalition commitment. The 2017–2021 coalition (Independence, Progressive, Left-Greens) maintained the suspension. The current government's decision to hold a referendum represented a compromise between pro-EU and anti-EU coalition partners. Post-referendum coalition dynamics will depend heavily on the result — a 'Yes' vote would make it difficult for anti-EU parties to block accession negotiations, while a 'No' vote would remove the issue from coalition politics for a generation.
caveats: Coalition dynamics are influenced by many issues beyond EU policy. The referendum's binding nature means that coalition manipulation of the EU question is limited post-vote. However, the pace and terms of accession negotiations (if approved) would still be determined by the government of the day.

related_entries.statement: Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) and Viðreisn (Reform Party) are the two Althingi parties that formally support EU membership. Samfylkingin has supported EU membership since 2007 and led the 2009 application process while in government. Viðreisn was founded in 2016 partly on a pro-EU platform and has consistently advocated for completing accession negotiations. Together they held approximately 17 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party), Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party), Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) oppose EU membership. The Independence Party has historically been the strongest opponent, citing sovereignty and fisheries concerns. The Progressive Party, traditionally representing rural interests, opposes membership primarily over agricultural and fisheries policy. These four parties collectively held approximately 33–35 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

citations.report_title: 25. þáttur Silfursins: Stefnt að kosningu um ESB í sumar

claims.claim_text: Í yfir tíu ár hefur þjóðin verið klofin um ESB-málið eftir að kosturinn var tekinn af borðinu.

citations.report_title: Enginn afsláttur gefinn segi þjóðin já við aðildarviðræðum

claims.claim_text: Allir flokkar ríkisstjórnarinnar voru sammála um að þjóðaratkvæðagreiðsla um ESB-aðildarviðræður yrði haldin, þrátt fyrir skiptar skoðanir innan og milli flokka.

citations.report_title: Flott embætti í boði fyrir ESB ríki

claims.claim_text: Samfylkingin, Viðreisn og Flokkur fólksins hafi sett ESB-aðild aftur á dagskrá með fyrirhugaðri þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu.
ESB-aðild Íslands hafi síðast verið á dagskrá undir stjórn Jóhönnu Sigurðardóttur, sem lagði fram aðildarumsókn árið 2009.
Sama ríkisstjórnin hafi ákveðið að gera hlé á aðildarviðræðunum árið 2013.
